docs.title: PyRailroad : Railroad-Diagram Generator
docs.text:
Generate Some railroad diagrams and more

PyRailroad is a Python package to draw railroad (or syntax) diagrams. Based largely on railroad-diagrams and the partial parser, both by tbatkins

This package can be used as a stand-alone (command-line interface) generator or as a library. this generates railroad diagrams (like what JSON.org uses) using SVG.

Railroad diagrams, or syntax diagrams, are useful in representing grammar in the programming sense.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
Install it with pip: python3 -m pip install pyrailroad

After that, py-railroad --help will show you some help.

This tool cas be used standalone as a command line program (CLI) or as a library. The former will give you details about the syntax of the input files or code used to generate the various diagram elements.

A Gallery is also coming soon to see how all the elements interact together.

docs.text:

SELECT column , FROM table , WHERE condition

repeat:

direction: left

text: ','
- element: Arrow

repeat:

items:
- element: Arrow
direction: left
- element: Terminal
text: ','
- element: Arrow

docs.title: v0.3.0
docs.text:
First release. This is on par with tbatkins' railroad.py with a couple fixes.

  • CLI functionality is mostly complete, and documented;
  • Parser for Bikeshed DSL
  • Parser for Json
  • Parser for YAML
  • library usage needs documentation.

docs.title: v0.3.1
docs.text: • Added an Arrow element to have diagrams with explicit orientation of paths.

docs.title: Using PyRailroad
docs.text:
Usage: py-railroad [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options: --help : shows the help message.

Commands:

  • dsl: parses a DSL FILE for railroad diagrams, based on significant whitespace and writes it into TARGET file.
  • json: parses a JSON FILE for railroad diagrams and writes it into TARGET file.
  • yaml: parses a YAML FILE for railroad diagrams and writes it into TARGET file.

For both json and yaml, various parameters of the diagram engine can be specified in a PARAMETERS file. For dsl, tow additional options exist:

  • --simple: draws the diagram using the "simple" style.
  • --standalone: embeds a default stylesheet inside the output for rendering.

Both styles are named so in the original code and haven't been changed (yet).

Examples:

DSLJSONYAML

Basic usage:

py-railroad dsl diagram_source.dsl diagram_output.svg

Generate a diagram using the simple style:

py-railroad dsl diagram_source.dsl diagram_output.svg --simple

Generate a standalone diagram:

py-railroad dsl diagram_source.dsl diagram_output.svg --standalone

Basic usage (default parameters):

py-railroad json diagram_source.json diagram_output.svg

Usage with a parameters file:

py-railroad json diagram_source.json diagram_output.svg custom_parameters.json

Basic usage (default parameters):

py-railroad yaml diagram_source.json diagram_output.svg

Usage with a parameters file:

py-railroad yaml diagram_source.yaml diagram_output.svg my_parameters.yaml

DSL support was initially developed by tbatkins in railroadparser.py and extended to support all the elements from railroad-diagrams that were missing. While complete in terms of elements, customization is limited to simple/complex styles and standalone or not. Several customizations of the diagram and of elements themselves are only available using the JSON or YAML parser.

docs.title: Diagram syntax
docs.text:
Diagrams are written in files using one of DSL, JSON or YAML languages. In this section we'll go over the various elements that go in a diagram and the output obtained. Unless otherwise noted, the output show is done using the simple style and no other parameter changed.

There are three categories of elements:

  • Base elements
  • Text elements
  • Block elements

docs.title: Base elements
docs.text: These are base diagram elements and are automatically added if omitted since they represent the whole diagram, start and end thereof respectively.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text: The root element of a railroad diagram is the Diagram element. If the input does not start with a Diagram, then one is automatically inserted at the root and will include all the elements in the input as its sub-elements.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
Diagram:
    ...

{
"element": "Diagram",
"items": [
...
]
}

element: Diagram
items:
- ...

docs.title: Start
docs.text: Start is an element that represents the start of the diagram. If it is not explicitly added, it will be inserted as the first element. It can take an optional label as a property with all three parsers, and an optional type property in JSON and YAML.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text:
• label: a string
  • type: one of simple, complex or sql. Setting this property at the element level will override what is specified in the parameter file.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Without a label

my label

With a label

With sql type

docs.title: End
docs.text: End is symmetric with Start and represents the end of the diagram. If it is not explicitly added, it will be inserted as the last element. It can take an optional type as a property in JSON and YAML.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text: • type: one of simple, complex or sql. Setting this property at the element level will override what is specified in the parameter file.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Without a label With sql type

docs.title: Block elements
docs.text: Block elements helps you structure your diagram logically and/or spatially. The spatial elements are: Sequence, Stack and Group; Logical elements are OptionalSequence, Choice, MultipleChoice, Optional, OneOrMore, ZeroOrMore, AlternatingSequence, and finally HorizontalChoice combines both spatial and logic.

docs.title: Sequence
docs.text: Sequence is a concatenation of other elements and requires at least one child element.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
Sequence:
    ...

{
"element": "Sequence",
"items": [
...
]
}

element: Sequence
items:
- ...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • items: an array/list of elements

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3

Sequence of three Terminals 1, 2 and 3

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: Identical to a Sequence, but the items are stacked vertically rather than horizontally. Best used when a simple Sequence would be too wide; instead, you can break the items up into a Stack of Sequences of an appropriate width.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
Stack:
    ...

{
"element": "Stack",
"items": [
...
]
}

element: Stack
items:
- ...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • items: an array/list of elements

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3

Stack of three Terminals 1, 2 and 3

docs.title: Group
docs.text: Highlights its child with a dashed outline, and optionally labels it. Passing a string as the label constructs a Comment, or you can build one yourself (to give an href or title). The DSL parser only accepts text labels, the JSON and YAML parsers accept any element as well as text.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text:
• label: optional, can be a string, or when using the JSON or YAML parsers, any element. The most likely case is using a Comment but any element will work.
  • item : a single element, mandatory.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo option 1 option 2 bar

Group with no label

foo option 1 option 2 DefinitionDefinition bar

Group with a (Comment) label

docs.title: Choice
docs.text: An exclusive choice among all branches.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML

Without default:

Choice:
    ...
    ...

With a default branch:

Choice: value
    ...
    ...

Without default:

{
"element": "Choice",
"items": [
...
...
]
}

With a default branch:

{
"element": "Choice",
"default": value,
"items": [
...
...
]
}

Without default:

element: Choice
items:
...
...

With a default branch:

element: Choice
default: value
items:
...
...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• default: int, optional (if not set: 0). Specifies which child is the "normal" choice and should go in the middle (starting from 0 for the first child).
  • items: an array/list of elements. Each element will have its own line.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3

Choice between three values

docs.title: HorizontalChoice
docs.text: Identical to Choice, but the items are stacked horizontally rather than vertically. There's no "straight-line" choice, so it just takes a list of children. Best used when a simple Choice would be too tall; instead, you can break up the items into a HorizontalChoice of Choices of an appropriate height.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
HorizontalChoice:
    ...
    ...

{
"element": "HorizontalChoice",
"items": [
...
...
]
}

element: HorizontalChoice
items:
...
...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • items: an array/list of elements. Each element will have its own "column".

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3 4 5 6

Choice between six values, broken in two blocks

docs.title: MultipleChoice
docs.text: Similar to a Choice, but more than one branch can be taken.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
MultipleChoice: value any|all
    ...
    ...

{
"element": "MultipleChoice",
"default": value,
"type": "any|all",
"items": [
...
...
]
}

element: MultipleChoice
default: value
type: any|all
items:
...
...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
All properties are mandatory.

  • default: int, specifies which child is the "normal" choice and should go in the middle
  • type: either any (1+ branches can be taken) or all (all branches must be taken).
  • items: an array/list of elements. Each element will have its own line.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3 take one or more branches, once each, in any order1+↺

MultipleChoice: any of three

1 2 3 take one or more branches, once each, in any order1+↺

MultipleChoice: all of three

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: A shorthand for Choice(0|1, Skip(), child).

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text:
• skip: with DSL, this is either empty or the string "skip" ; in JSON/YAML, this is an optional boolean (false if not specified).
  • item: an element, mandatory.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo

Optional without skip

foo

Optional with skip

docs.title: OptionalSequence
docs.text:
A Sequence where every item is individually optional, but at least one item must be chosen.

DSLJSONYAML 
OptionalSequence:
    ...

{
"element": "OptionalSequence",
"items": [
...
]
}

element: OptionalSequence
items:
- ...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • items: an array/list of elements

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
1 2 3

OptionalSequence of three Terminals 1, 2 and 3

docs.title: OneOrMore
docs.text: A loop that requires taking the first element at least once. The loop is typically a Comment but can be any element.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text:
• item : a single element, mandatory.
  • repeat: if empty, will just draw a line, else will insert the element on the loop. With the DSL parser, this is a string.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo

Simple OneOrMore

foo bar

OneOrMore with a label or Comment

Sequence( element , )

OneOrMore with a Terminal (",")

docs.title: ZeroOrMore
docs.text: A shorthand for Optional(OneOrMore(child, repeat), skip). Like OneOrMore, this is a loop, but it can be skipped.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text:
• item : a single element, mandatory.
  • repeat: if omitted, will just draw a line, else will insert the element on the loop. With the DSL parser, this is a string.
  • skip: with DSL, this is either empty or the string "skip" ; in JSON/YAML, this is an optional boolean (false if not specified).

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo

Simple ZeroOrMore

foo bar

ZeroOrMore with skip and a label

docs.title: AlternatingSequence
docs.text: Similar to a OneOrMore, where you must alternate between the two choices, but allows you to start and end with either element (OneOrMore requires you to start and end with the "child" node).

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML 
AlternatingSequence:
    ...
    ...

{
"element": "AlternatingSequence",
"items": [
...
...
]
}

element: AlternatingSequence
items:
- ...
- ...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • items: an array/list of exactly two elements

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo bar

AlternatingSequence

docs.title: Text elements
docs.text: Text elements are single elements on the diagram and the base building blocks. They are either Terminal, NonTerminal, Comment, Arrow or Skip.

docs.title: Terminal
docs.text: Terminal represents literal text. The Terminal element has a required property text, and three optional properties href, title and cls. The last two properties are only available with the JSON and YAML parsers.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML

Basic syntax:

Terminal: my text

With a href:

Terminal https://github.com: github

Basic syntax:

{
"element": "Terminal",
"text": "my text"
}

With href:

{
"element": "Terminal",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com"
}

With additional options:

{
"element": "Terminal",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com",
"title": "This is a link",
"cls": "custom_terminal"
}

Without a label:

element: Terminal
text: my text

With href

element: Terminal
text: github
href: https://github.com

With additional options:

element: Terminal
text: github
href: https://github.com
title: This is a link
cls: custom_terminal

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• text: string, required
  • href: string, optional
  • title: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers
  • cls: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
my text

Simple Terminal

githubgithub

With href

githubThis is a link

With additional options (hover for the title)

docs.title: NonTerminal
docs.text: NonTerminal represents another production or diagram. The NonTerminal element has a required property text, and three optional properties href, title and cls. The last two properties are only available with the JSON and YAML parsers.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML

Basic syntax:

NonTerminal: my text

With a href:

NonTerminal https://github.com: github

Basic syntax:

{
"element": "NonTerminal",
"text": "my text"
}

With href:

{
"element": "NonTerminal",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com"
}

With additional options:

{
"element": "NonTerminal",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com",
"title": "This is a link",
"cls": "custom_terminal"
}

Without a label:

element: NonTerminal
text: my text

With href

element: NonTerminal
text: github
href: https://github.com

With additional options:

element: NonTerminal
text: github
href: https://github.com
title: This is a link
cls: custom_terminal

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• text: string, required
  • href: string, optional
  • title: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers
  • cls: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
my text

Simple Terminal

githubgithub

With href

githubgithubThis is a link

With additional options (hover for the title)

docs.title: Comment
docs.text: Represents a comment. The Comment element has a required property text, and three optional properties href, title and cls. The last two properties are only available with the JSON and YAML parsers.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
DSLJSONYAML

Basic syntax:

Comment: my text

With a href:

Comment https://github.com: github

Basic syntax:

{
"element": "Comment",
"text": "my text"
}

With href:

{
"element": "Comment",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com"
}

With additional options:

{
"element": "Comment",
"text": "github",
"href": "https://github.com",
"title": "This is a link",
"cls": "custom_terminal"
}

Without a label:

element: Comment
text: my text

With href

element: Comment
text: github
href: https://github.com

With additional options:

element: Comment
text: github
href: https://github.com
title: This is a link
cls: custom_terminal

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• text: string, required
  • href: string, optional
  • title: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers
  • cls: string, optional, only available with the JSON and YAML parsers

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
my text

Simple Comment

githubgithub

With href

githubgithubThis is a link

With additional options (hover for the title)

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text: direction: optional string, can be left or right to orient the arrow left or right, or any other string to draw a plain line instead.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Arrow left Arrow right Undirected: a line

docs.title: Skip
docs.text: An empty line. Used for vertical blocks like Stack.

docs.title: Syntax
Properties
docs.text: This element has no properties.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
foo foo

Stack without Skip

foo foo

Stack with Skip
